title: ledonia peduncularis var. salviifolia
side_effects: Ledonia peduncularis var. salviifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins. Prolonged use of the plant may also lead to kidney damage and reduced blood flow to vital organs. In some cases, improper use of Ledonia peduncularis var. salviifolia can cause allergic reactions, resulting in skin irritation, respiratory problems, and increased heart rate. Furthermore, excessive consumption of the plant may interact with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
